input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is dealing with a known behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MSTest). In Microsoft.Testing.Platform, when a test assembly produces zero tests, it returns exit code 8 (which means "no tests found"), causing CI pipelines to fail. With VSTest, this was not an error.

Let me look at their project files to understand the current setup.

arguments.new_str:
<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8). -->
    <NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>true</NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>

<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8). -->
    <NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>true</NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>

data.content: Wait — I used the wrong property. Let me fix that.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8). -->
    <NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>true</NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
Wait, I need to double-check my answer. The correct MSBuild property for Microsoft.Testing.Platform to treat "no tests found" as a non-error is not `NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration`. Let me think about this more carefully.

In Microsoft.Testing.Platform, exit code 8 means "zero tests ran." The correct way to handle this is:

1. **`--ignore-exit-code`** command-line option (e.g., `dotnet test --ignore-exit-code 8`)
2. **MSBuild property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests`** - but I'm not sure this exists


3. **`--minimum-expected-tests 0`** command-line argument - this defaults to 1, which is why exit code 8 gets triggered when no tests are found

So the real solution is to either use `--minimum-expected-tests 0` to allow zero tests without error, or use `--ignore-exit-code 8` to suppress that specific exit code. I can set this through an MSBuild property in the project file using `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments`, or more cleanly through a `.testingplatformconfig.json` file. For `dotnet test` integration, I can pass the argument directly to configure the minimum expected tests threshold.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8). -->
    <NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>true</NoDefaultTestHostContainerReceiverRegistration>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: Let me check the log file to see what happened.

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` rejects `0`. But the help output shows `--ignore-exit-code 8` is the right option — it maps exit code 8 (no tests found) to 0.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat "no tests found" as success (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The `--minimum-expected-tests` option rejects `0` as a value — it requires "a single non-zero positive integer value." So that approach doesn't work.

However, I can see from the help output that there's an `--ignore-exit-code` option that can ignore specific exit codes:

```
--ignore-exit-code
    Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes
    (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)
```

So the fix is to use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead. Let me update the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat "no tests found" as success (exit 0 instead of exit 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**The fix:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). Adding:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

to your `<PropertyGroup>` maps exit code 8 back to 0, restoring the VSTest behavior for assemblies that produce no tests due to conditional compilation. Actual test failures (exit code 2) are still reported normally.
